sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# After clicking the green arrow, more blocks changed color. Let me see what changed.
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame
before_ascii = before.ascii.split('\n')
after_ascii = after.ascii.split('\n')
for i in range(len(after_ascii)):
    b_row = before_ascii[i] if i < len(before_ascii) else ''
    a_row = after_ascii[i] if i < len(after_ascii) else ''
    if b_row != a_row:
        print("Row %d changed" % i)
        print("  Before: " + b_row[:65])
        print("  After:  " + a_row[:65])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicking the green arrow shifted colors in the right-side blocks. It seems like clicking arrows shift colors of blocks in their row. Let me see the current block colors.
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
blocks = []
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] == 16:
        b = n['boundary']
        center_r = (b[0][0] + b[2][0]) // 2
        center_c = (b[0][1] + b[2][1]) // 2
        blocks.append((center_r, center_c, n['color']))
blocks.sort()
for r, c, color in blocks:
    print("  (%d, %d): %s" % (r, c, color))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

initial_frame = history[0].frame
initial_seg = initial_frame.segmentation
initial_blocks = []
for n in initial_seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 16:
        b = n['boundary']
        center_r = (b[0][0] + b[2][0]) // 2
        center_c = (b[0][1] + b[2][1]) // 2
        initial_blocks.append((center_r, center_c, n['color']))
initial_blocks.sort()
print("Initial blocks:")
for r, c, color in initial_blocks:
    print("  (%d, %d): %s" % (r, c, color))
